config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that easily becomes a justification for trampling basic rights in the name of “the greater good.” In my view, a healthy society protects personal liberty, dignity, and due process first, then balances them with public interests when there is a real and clear need. From my perspective, once individual rights become too weak, society becomes unfair and people lose trust in it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman nilai-nilai Amerika, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari negara bukan hambatan bagi masyarakat; justru itulah yang membuat masyarakat tetap adil dan dipercaya. Tentu ada keadaan darurat, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, ketika kepentingan bersama bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan sementara. Kalau tidak, “demi masyarakat” sering hanya menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia that idea can too easily be used to silence dissent and excuse abuse by authorities. Our history shows that when “public interest” is invoked without strong limits, ordinary people often bear the cost while those in power avoid accountability. I do think there are limited cases, like disease control or emergency security, where some rights can be restricted, but only if the restriction is necessary, proportionate, and temporary. Without that, society becomes an excuse to crush the very people it is supposed to serve.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai sebagai prioritas otomatis, karena dalam pengalaman Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi pagar utama agar negara tidak semena-mena atas nama “kepentingan umum.” Argumen bahwa masyarakat bisa mengorbankan hak demi kebaikan bersama memang terdengar masuk akal, tetapi terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, terutama terhadap kelompok yang lemah atau tidak populer. Saya setuju ada keadaan darurat tertentu, seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan nyata, di mana pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sempit, terukur, dan sementara. Di luar itu, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu karena justru dari sanalah keadilan dan kepercayaan publik tumbuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in Indonesia that phrase is often used to pressure people into silence and obedience. I have seen how “for the public good” can become a convenient shield for officials or institutions to avoid accountability, while ordinary people lose their voice. Yes, in emergencies like disease outbreaks or real security threats, some rights can be limited, but only if the limits are narrow, temporary, and clearly justified. A society is not truly strong if it protects order while weakening the dignity and rights of the people inside it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika pun saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan negara melampaui batas. Argumen bahwa hak individu menghalangi kebaikan bersama terdengar rapi di atas kertas, tetapi dalam praktik sering paling merugikan orang yang sudah rentan atau tidak populer. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan nyata, tetapi pembatasan harus benar-benar sempit, terukur, dan sementara, bukan alasan untuk memperluas kuasa pemerintah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu dulu, lalu menyeimbangkannya secara ketat hanya saat ada kebutuhan yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
